name: Castile Soap
description: A true soap made from saponified plant oils, producing a biodegradable surfactant capable of replacing body wash, hand soap, face wash, and household cleaners.
chemicalName: Mixture of potassium/sodium salts of fatty acids (potassium oleate, potassium laurate, potassium palmitate)
appearance: Liquid: amber to brown viscous liquid. Bar: white to cream solid.
solubility: Fully soluble in water; forms micelles above critical micelle concentration
chemistry: True soap from saponified plant oils. Surfactant with hydrophilic/lipophilic ends. Biodegradable. Alkaline pH (~9).
warnings: Don't mix with acids. Hard water = soap scum.
shelfLife: 3+ years (liquid); bar soap improves with age
